author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
viśve udita, nāma⋅tapana,
avidyā⋅vināśa lāgi’
choḍata saba, māyā⋅vibhava,
sādhu tāhe anurāgī

harināma⋅prabhākara, avidyā⋅timirahara,
tomāra mahimā kebā jāne
ke hena paṇḍitajana, tomāra māhātmyagaṇa,
uccaiḥsvare sakala bākhāne

tomāra ābhāsa pahilahi bhāya
e bhava⋅timira kavalitaprāya

acire timira nāśiyā prajñāna
tattvāndha⋅nayane karena vidhāna

sei ta’ prajñāna viśuddhā bhakati
upajāya hari⋅viṣayiṇī mati

e adbhutalīlā satata tomāra
bhakativinoda jāniyāche sāra
en_translation:
Risen in the world, the sun of the Holy Name destroys ignorance. Giving up all the opulence of illusion, the saintly soul becomes devoted to it.

O Holy Name of Hari! You are the sun, the dispeller of the darkness of ignorance. Who indeed knows Your glory? What learned person can recount Your many glories and proclaim them all in a loud voice?

Your glimmer first appears, and the darkness of this material existence is almost swallowed.

Quickly destroying the darkness, supreme knowledge bestows sight upon the eyes that were blind to reality.

That supreme knowledge is indeed pure devotion, which awakens an awakens an inclination focused on the Lord.

This wonderful pastime is always Yours; Bhaktivinoda has understood its essence.
